Amazon in the community: Service, community, and commitment at HQ2

Learn how we invest in the community, help power the local economy, and support our employees in the National Capital Region.

    Written by Amazon Staff

    Recent Updates
    June 5, 2026 12:52 PM

    From 20 riders to 250: How Amazon’s cycling community showed up for the Armed Forces Cycling Classic

    When Logan Cunningham completed her first Olympic-distance triathlon race at 13 years old, she never imagined she’d one day lead a 250-person corporate team through the streets of Arlington. But that’s exactly what happened this year at the Amazon Armed Forces Cycling Classic, the Capital region’s premier cycling event and one of the largest races in the D.C. area.
    Amazon has served as the title sponsor since 2025. This year marked a milestone: a new partnership with Amaury Sport Organisation (A.S.O.), the company behind the Tour de France, elevating the event from a regional tradition to one with international recognition.
    The weekend kicked off Saturday with the Challenge Ride, open to cycling enthusiasts of all ages and abilities who participated on behalf of a corporate team, an armed forces branch, or as individuals. Sunday brought the beloved Clarendon Cup, the D.C. region’s premier professional and amateur race since 1998.
    Amazon boasts the largest corporate team in the event—a community that has grown more than tenfold under Logan Cunningham’s leadership.
    “I’m so glad I get to bring my passion for cycling to Amazon and build a cycling community among my fellow Amazonians,” said Cunningham. “It has been amazing seeing our corporate team grow from 20 riders when we first started to the more than 250 that participated this year.”
    The event reflects Amazon’s broader commitment to the communities surrounding HQ2, supporting military service members, bringing employees together outside the workplace, and investing in the vibrancy of the Capital region.
    “Amazon is proud of our cycling culture and to be the lead sponsor of this incredible event that supports our military, engages our workforce, and strengthens communities,” said Steve Hartell, Amazon’s vice president of public policy. “We look forward to continuing to support the Armed Forces Cycling Classic and deepening our commitment to the Capital region.”

    5 ways Amazon is investing in Virginia

    Amazon's investments are boosting the Commonwealth’s economy while creating opportunities for Virginians.

    May 29, 2026 1:34 PM

    Amazon partners with Axios to discuss the future of AI in the US workforce

    Photo credit Frankie Luis Garcia for Axios
    Earlier this month, Amazon partnered with Axios Live to host Future of Work: Succeeding in the AI Era at our National Landing campus. Axios senior economics reporter Courtenay Brown and senior tech policy reporter Ashley Gold moderated conversations on the intersectional roles of public-private partnerships, workforce development, and education in the age of Artificial Intelligence.
    Panelists included:
    ● Holly Sullivan, Amazon’s vice president of worldwide economic development policy
    ● The Honorable Sen. Todd Young
    ● Dr. Anne M. Kress, Northern Virginia Community College President
    The discussion explored how policymakers, educators, and the private sector are collaborating to develop training programs that equip workers for new and not-yet defined roles—and how institutions like NOVA are evolving to prepare the next generation for an AI-driven economy.
    Events like this reflect Amazon’s commitment to convening leaders across sectors to advance real solutions from community college pipelines to federal AI policy. We were proud to host this timely conversation at HQ2 and look forward to bringing more events like this to National Landing.
    May 21, 2026 2:30 PM

    Amazon hosts community book fair at Met Park, unveils Little Library

    On Saturday, May 16, Amazon hosted a Community Book Fair at Met Park on the campus of HQ2 in National Landing, Virginia. Hundreds of community members came together for a free morning of celebrating books, reading, and math for kids.

    A bestselling author, free books, and a sweet surprise

    New York Times bestselling author and mathematician Danica McKellar led live readings for children in attendance, and every kid went home with a copy of her book, Goodnight, Numbers. The event also featured crafts, Toby's Ice Cream, and Amazon bananas to fuel the fun.
    The book fair was produced in collaboration with E3: Elevate Early Education, the READ, VA campaign, and Dolly Parton's Imagination Library of Northern Virginia.

    A lasting investment in the community

    Amazon also announced the installation of a new Little Library on the HQ2 campus, stocked with books from Dolly Parton's Imagination Library. The Little Library will provide free, year-round access to books for children and families in the National Landing community—extending the spirit of the book fair well beyond a single Saturday morning.
    May 11, 2026 9:57 AM

    Amazon and DDOT launch e-cargo bike pilot in Washington, D.C.

    Amazon Cargo E-Bikes in 20003 zip code on April 28, 2026 in Washington, District of Columbia.
    Amazon is working with the District Department of Transportation (DDOT) to test e-cargo bike deliveries in Washington, D.C.

    How does the e-cargo bike pilot work?

    Through the new pilot program, Delivery Service Partners (DSPs) will deliver Amazon packages to customers across the District using a fleet of up to 15 battery-powered e-cargo bikes that move quickly, safely, and quietly through busy city streets.
    E-cargo bikes are a natural fit for urban deliveries. With a smaller footprint than cars or vans, they can operate in dedicated bike lanes, helping reduce traffic congestion and noise. The e-cargo bikes have four wheels and a secure rear cargo hold, along with covered seating, a windshield, and windshield wipers, providing weather protection for DSP delivery associates.

    Why is Amazon testing e-cargo bikes in D.C.?

    "We've seen the benefits e-cargo bikes bring to urban deliveries around the world, and we're excited to launch this pilot alongside DDOT to help ensure we get it right here in D.C.," said Dave Alperson, vice president of North America last mile transportation at Amazon. "We'll continue working alongside community partners in the National Capital Region who understand the local needs best—combining their insights with our expertise in delivery, technology, and innovation to make a real difference in people's everyday lives."

    Where else does Amazon use e-cargo bikes?

    Amazon has been investing in micromobility like e-cargo bikes and on-foot deliveries across the globe, including in New York City andmore than 50 cities in Europe. The D.C. pilot is the latest chapter in that work, and Amazon looks forward to working closely with DDOT as the company continues to expand safe and efficient deliveries for our neighbors in the National Capital Region.

    Amazon now has more than 50,000 electric delivery vans globally. Meet the growing lineup of electric vehicles.

    From custom-built vans to e-cargo bikes, these zero-tailpipe-emission vehicles help deliver billions of packages to customers around the world.

    April 8, 2026 3:07 PM

    Spring has arrived at Amazon HQ2, and we’re celebrating in full color.

    National Cherry Blossom FestivalPhoto by Jason Dixson Photography
    This year, Amazon is proud to be part of the National Cherry Blossom Festival, and the celebration has been in full swing across the region. On March 28, Amazon brought the National Mall to life as the presenting sponsor of the Blossom Kite Festival, which brought together families for an afternoon of kite making and live music. Back in National Landing, Pink in the Pool kicked off the season on March 21 with free access to the Long Bridge Aquatics Center and Japanese cultural activities like tea ceremonies, origami, and paper lantern decorating.
    The best part? There’s still more to come.

    Pink in the Park returns for its fourth year

    Amazon is co-presenting Pink in the Park with the National Landing BID, now in its fourth year as Virginia’s premier celebration of the National Cherry Blossom Festival. The series continues with Pink Beats on April 9 at the Water Park, before coming to a close on April 10 with Art in the Park at Metropolitan Park--an after-hours showcase featuring 50+ local artists, Japanese and pan-Asian cuisine, and DJ entertainment from 5 p.m. to 10 p.m.
    “Amazon is proud to support Pink in the Park, a vibrant, community-driven tradition that continues to grow each year,” said Patrick Phillippi, head of community engagement for the National Capital Region at Amazon. “We’re excited to welcome neighbors back to Metropolitan Park for the Art of Pink exhibition and to spotlight the creativity of local artists and businesses. It’s a joyful way to celebrate spring and the spirit of this city together.”
    From the Washington Monument Grounds to Metropolitan Park, Amazon is honored to help bring the Cherry Blossom Festival to life on both sides of the river. Register for Art Park to join us on April 10.

    A guide to Amazon Literary Partnership grants—what to know and how to apply

    Applications are now open for 2026 grants supporting nonprofit literary organizations that empower writers across all genres.

    April 1, 2026 9:00 AM

    Building community together at HQ2 and Virginia

    2026 marks a milestone year for Amazon in Virginia. As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in the Commonwealth and approach the opening of Arlington Community High School at Met Park this fall, we’re proud to reflect on how our partnership with Virginia has grown beyond traditional economic development into a model of community-centered investment.

    Met Park brings jobs, small businesses, and community programming

    Since opening Met Park in May 2023, nearly 8,500 Amazon employees now work at our second headquarters. The campus includes 50,000 square feet of retail space for 13 local small businesses and a 2.5-acre public park that has hosted hundreds of community events—from affordable housing summits to farmers markets, live music, and art programming with MoCA Arlington’s Innovation Studio—reaching an estimated 133,000 community members.
    Since implementing our 5-day return-to-office policy in January 2025, we've continued to see positive economic impacts in the National Landing community, including foot traffic averaged more than 155,000 monthly visitors at Met Park.

    $1.3 billion investment in affordable housing

    Our commitment extends far beyond job creation. Through Amazon’s housing fund, we’ve invested $1.3 billion to preserve and create over 10,000 affordable housing units across the National Capital Region. In February, we broke ground on the Victory Center in Alexandria—transforming a long-vacant office building into 377 all-affordable homes. We’ve also donated more than $60 million to 230+ local community partners in the last 12 months.

    Building Virginia's talent pipeline

    We’re building Virginia’s talent pipeline from the ground up, investing more than $5.7 million in CodeVA to expand computer science education and awarding $920,000 in Amazon Future Engineer Scholarships to Virginia students. Over 5,000 Amazon operations employees in Virginia have participated in Career Choice, our pre-paid tuition program.
    Virginia is a great state to do business. Across the Commonwealth, we continue to invest in our corporate office in Herndon, our fulfillment network including the launch of ORF4—our robotics fulfillment center in Virginia Beach, and in AI and cloud infrastructure that powers businesses statewide. As we enter this milestone year, Amazon remains committed to being a good employer, neighbor, and community partner in Virginia.
    March 4, 2026 9:47 AM

    Amazon breaks ground on 377 all-affordable homes in Alexandria's West End

    Representatives from Amazon and Stonebridge joined Alexandria Mayor Alyia Gaskins and City of Alexandria leadership at the groundbreaking ceremony for 377 all-affordable homes at 5001 Eisenhower Avenue.
    Amazon, Mayor Alyia Gaskins, and developer Stonebridge today broke ground on the Victory Center at 5001 Eisenhower Avenue—a formerly vacant 11-story office building in Alexandria, Virginia, that will be transformed into 377 all-affordable homes by 2027.

    A building reimagined

    When this project was first conceived, it was planned as a mixed-affordability development. Amazon's investment changed that. Today, every one of the 377 homes will be affordable—235 of them two- and three-bedroom units for families—with half available to renters earning 50% to 60% of area median income and half to those earning 80% of area median income. Amazon's participation also extended the affordability commitment from 40 years to 99 years.
    "This project represents something bigger than just one building," said Steve Hartell, vice president of public policy at Amazon. "It will help chart a course for a new future—not only for the generations of residents who will call this place home, but for the broader Eisenhower West redevelopment as well."

    Built for Alexandria's essential workers

    With 235 family-sized units and easy Metro access, 5001 Eisenhower Avenue is designed for Alexandria's essential workers and families who want to stay in the neighborhoods they love.
    "I am proud to be the leader of a city where we have been so tirelessly committed to building affordable housing," said Mayor Alyia Gaskins of Alexandria, Virginia. "Making sure that we are building housing options everywhere and in every part of our community so that anyone who wants to live here is able to live here and stay here."

    Part of a larger commitment

    Today's groundbreaking is part of Amazon's commitment of more than $1.3 billion to create and preserve over 10,000 affordable homes in the National Capital Region. In Alexandria, Amazon has committed over $166 million to support more than 1,400 affordable homes—part of our $3.6 billion Housing Fund spanning the Puget Sound region, National Capital Region, and Nashville.
    March 2, 2026 11:26 AM

    Amazon supports Fairfax County students in Presidential AI Challenge

    Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) students showcased innovative AI solutions this week as part of the Seize the Moment Student AI Innovation Challenge, a program supported by Amazon through its work with Playlab.
    Top student teams from 25 FCPS high schools demonstrated custom AI apps at Herndon High School, just miles from Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, Virginia. Students used Playlab's sandboxed AI environment to create AI-powered apps designed to tackle real-world challenges, including improving government policies and helping international students transition.
    The evening concluded with five finalist teams presenting their apps to a panel of judges from Amazon, FCPS, and the Virginia Department of Education.
    "It's inspiring to see students across Fairfax County and throughout the country build innovative solutions to address challenges in their classrooms and communities," said Valerie Singer, general manager of global education at Amazon Web Services. "Amazon is proud to support STEM education for students of all ages, and we're excited to partner with Playlab on the Presidential AI Challenge. Together, we're helping students safely use AI to learn and create."
    This effort is part of Amazon's support for AI education and literacy for learners and workers. This broad work includes support for the Presidential AI Challenge and the Pledge to America's Youth, as well as Amazon's Future 2030—a $2.5 billion commitment to expand access to education and skills training to help prepare at least 50 million people for the future of work.

    STEM Education programs Amazon supports in the U.S.

    We are committed to increase youth and adult access to STEM opportunities, inspire students to explore careers of the future and the broad range of technical and non-technical roles available to them, and promote diversity and inclusion in the tech industry.

    February 20, 2026 2:14 PM

    Amazon joins National Innovation Quarter as founding partner in National Landing

    Photo by Connor McLaren Photography/Connor McLaren Photography
    Amazon is proud to call National Landing home. With its cutting-edge workforce, diverse and growing suite of industries and amenities, and proximity to federal policymakers and global institutions, it is no surprise that National Landing is a driver of regional economic growth. Since establishing HQ2, we have seen how bringing leaders together in the right place generates breakthroughs in national competitiveness and technology.

    Building innovation beyond HQ2

    Our efforts to build the future don’t stop at HQ2. We are investing in community organizations and programs that strengthen collaboration and tackle some of the most pressing issues facing our communities. In National Landing, this commitment includes our role as a Founding Partner for the new National Innovation Quarter—National IQ.
    Launched this month, National IQ is a landmark nonprofit organization that brings together leaders from industry, academia, government, and entrepreneurship to amplify the area’s innovation ecosystem.

    Industry leaders join forces in National Landing

    By tapping into the public and private sector organizations across Crystal City, Pentagon City, and Potomac Yard, National IQ will strengthen collaboration and solidify the community as a global innovation hub. The impact of this initiative will be felt immediately with the rollout of year-round programming, initiatives, and startup and innovation accelerators.
    Marked by a launch event at the National Landing Experience Center on February 11, Amazon joined other Founding Partners and early sponsors to celebrate National IQ’s role as a catalyst for innovation and growth for the region and the nation.
    “National IQ creates a powerful platform to accelerate this innovation ecosystem,” said Brian Stout, senior manager of U.S. public policy at Amazon. “It brings the right people together, with the right resources, in the right place, and that combination is what drives breakthrough solutions. We’re excited to see the breakthroughs that emerge when this community comes together.”

    Amazon continues to be one of the world’s leading corporate purchasers of carbon-free energy

    With more than 700 projects globally, Amazon is investing in over 40 gigawatts of carbon-free energy, which is enough to power more than 12.1 million U.S. homes.

    January 2, 2026 2:42 PM

    Amazon celebrates HQ2 community impact: $60 million donated to more than 230 local partners in 2025

    In December, Amazon employees gathered at the company’s Amazon Visitor Landing in HQ2 for a celebration of community impact.
    Brian Huseman, Amazon’s vice president of public policy and community engagement, welcomed familiar faces and new colleagues alike to reflect on the ways Amazon is investing in the region. “You’re the ones volunteering at local schools, mentoring students, supporting food banks, and showing up for our community partners.” Huseman told Amazon employees. “You understand what it means to be a good neighbor here. And you’re our best ambassadors—the people who can authentically share Amazon’s story because you are part of it.”
    Tracy Sayegh Gabriel, chief executive officer of the National Landing Business Improvement District, moderated a conversation with Holly Sullivan, vice president of worldwide economic development policy, and Brian Kenner, global head of geographic giving and community engagement. The conversation explored Amazon’s economic contributions in Virginia and hyperlocal giving strategy that has made HQ2 a model for community investment.
    With 8,500 employees at HQ2 and over 66,000 across Virginia, Maryland, and Washington, D.C., Amazon is creating meaningful economic and community benefits. In 2025 alone, Amazon donated over $60 million to more than 230 local community partners throughout the DMV.
    A Q&A panel featuring Sullivan, Kenner, Senthil Sankaran (managing principal of Amazon’s housing fund), and Bettina Stix (global director of Amazon Community Impact) highlighted the company’s comprehensive approach in the region: the creation and preservation of more than 10,000 affordable homes supporting more than 22,000 residents, more than 1.5 million meals delivered to local organizations and families, and more than 700,000 student hours of free STEM education provided.
    Amazon employees are at the heart of this work. In 2025, more than 2,200 HQ2 employees participated in more than 270 volunteer events, supporting over 100 community organizations. This work represents Amazon’s Leadership Principles in action, demonstrating that success and scale bring broad responsibility to the communities we call home.
    Read more about Amazon’s community impact in the National Capital Region.
    December 1, 2025 1:19 PM

    Holiday magic at HQ2: Art, community, and local flavor at Met Park

    The holiday season is in full swing at HQ2, and Metropolitan Park is becoming a destination where art, local businesses, and community experiences come together. These moments reflect the spirit of what we are building in National Landing and the partnerships that continue to grow around our campus.
    From December 1 through January 25, visitors can explore Horizon, an immersive pop-up art installation by Creos and artist Olivier Landreville. The experience features five egg-shaped cocoons designed to create a sense of calm. Guests can sit back, gently rock, and look up into starry skies, bird formations, or a soothing leafy canopy. Soft lighting, natural wood textures, and layered soundscapes create a moment of quiet in the heart of HQ2. Horizon adds to Amazon's commitment to supporting public art and cultural programming that builds community at Met Park.
    On December 6, the National Landing BID, in partnership with Amazon, brings The Lodge to Met Park with a full lineup of seasonal activities that also spotlight local businesses and artists. Neighbors, families, and four-legged friends can enjoy gingerbread chalet building inside the MoCA Arlington Innovation Studio, along with selfies with Santa, face painting, letters to Santa, ornament making, and Ski Vacation Bingo under covered tents on the Central Green. Visitors can also take part in the Peppermint Mocha Latte Competition which features local coffee shops—including Good Company Coffee, one of several small businesses supported through HQ2's engagement and vendor partnership efforts.
    These collaborations with the BID and our neighborhood retailers help activate Met Park, create economic opportunities, and reinforce HQ2 as a welcoming, community-centered destination.
    November 28, 2025 11:10 AM

    Cloud Connect brings university talent to HQ2 for hands-on learning and mentorship

    On November 14, Amazon's Black Employee Network (BEN Tech) partnered with Amazon University Talent Acquisition and Field North America Tech Customer Service teams to welcome more than 120 university students to HQ2 for Cloud Connect. The event gave students an inside look at Amazon while helping them connect their academic interests to real career paths.
    Students attended from across the region, representing more than 10 colleges and universities including Howard University, University of Maryland, University of Virginia, Virginia Tech, George Mason University, Morgan State, and Carnegie Mellon. Their studies covered more than 15 majors such as computer science, cybersecurity, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and business. Student organizations were also represented, including the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), Society of Women Engineers (SWE), ColorStack, and the Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE).
    The day began with a panel featuring senior leaders who shared the turns their careers have taken and the impact their teams have on the technology that powers Amazon. Their stories opened the door for meaningful conversations and set the tone for the hands-on learning ahead.
    Following the senior leader panel, students joined a careers in tech conversation and a recruiting workshop before heading into the PartyRock workshop. There, they built generative AI applications in teams and presented them in a Shark Tank style pitch session. The room quickly shifted into a creative space filled with notes, sketches, and teammates working together to bring their concepts to life.
    To keep that momentum going, each student left paired with a mentor who will support them as they navigate school, internships, and early career decisions. More than 65 Amazon volunteers helped create an environment where students and employees could connect and learn from one another.
    Cloud Connect not only introduced students to opportunities at Amazon, it built relationships that strengthen the connection between Amazon teams and the communities we serve.
    November 24, 2025 1:05 PM

    Amazon and Horning Partners open Plaza Towers, adding 288 affordable homes to National Capital Region

    When Plaza Towers opened its doors this week in Hyattsville, Maryland, it brought hundreds of affordable apartments to Prince George's County, and what makes this even more impactful is that they will remain affordable for generations to come.
    The 288-unit development, built by Horning Partners with $18.72 million in low-cost financing from Amazon, held its ribbon-cutting ceremony with community members and local officials. For many attendees, the building represents more than new construction—it's a chance for families to stay in a region where housing costs have pushed many out.
    "We created our housing fund for projects just like this," said Senthil Sankaran, managing principal of Amazon's housing fund. "Working alongside partners like Horning and County Executive Braveboy, we're helping more families access housing they can afford."
    The location offers residents easy access to transit lines, employment centers, and community resources—factors that can make the difference between struggling and thriving for families on tight budgets.
    Plaza Towers is part of Amazon's broader effort in the National Capital Region, where the company has committed over $1.3 billion to help preserve and create more than 10,000 affordable homes. Other projects include the Somos community at McLean Metro, The Chimes in North Bethesda, and sustainable housing developments in Washington, D.C. Together, these initiatives aim to benefit more than 22,000 residents across the region.
    As housing affordability continues to challenge communities nationwide, developments like Plaza Towers show what's possible when developers, local government, and private partners align their resources toward a common goal.

    How Amazon is helping communities in Jamaica impacted by Hurricane Melissa

    Amazon is working closely with UN agencies and other first responders as Melissa threatens widespread devastation.

    November 6, 2025 9:53 AM

    Amazon glows green to honor veterans through Operation Green Light

    This week, Amazon sites across the country are glowing green—a simple yet powerful symbol of appreciation for veterans, active-duty service members, military families, and those who support them.
    As part of the fifth annual Operation Green Light for Veterans, led by the National Association of Counties (NACo) and the National Association of County Veterans Service Officers (NACVSO), Amazon is illuminating more than 200 sites and buildings nationwide from November 4–11. The initiative honors the contributions of veterans and their families and shines a light on the importance of gratitude in our communities.
    "Operation Green Light is an opportunity to thank veterans for both their service to our nation and their contributions to our communities," said NACo Executive Director Matthew Chase. "With hundreds of counties coming together to light up everything from courthouses to bridges, we hope veterans will see and feel our gratitude for their selflessness and courage. We thank Amazon for their continued support and partnership in helping honor and uplift those who have served."
    At HQ2, this effort reflects Amazon's commitment to being a good employer and neighbor. With more than 6,500 members across the D.C., Maryland, and Virginia area, our employee-led group Warriors at Amazon embodies the spirit of service—supporting one another and giving back long after their time in uniform.
    "What makes Operation Green Light powerful isn't just the color, it's the connection. Across more than 600 chapters and 20,000 members globally, Amazon's Warriors network continues to unite Amazon employees who lead with gratitude and show up with purpose. This moment is a thank-you to every veteran, family member, and ally who stands in support of the military community, today and every day," said David Sopczak, president of Warriors at Amazon.
    To our veterans, service members, and the Warriors at Amazon community—thank you for your courage, leadership, and the light you bring to Amazon every day.

    Our impact in the HQ2 region

    When we announced that our second headquarters would be coming to Arlington, Virginia, in 2018, we made a commitment to be among the most trusted companies in the region. From boosting local jobs and small businesses to expanding affordable housing and supporting Virginia nonprofits, transit, and community projects, we are proud to call Virginia home and to continue being a great neighbor.

    November 6, 2025 8:59 AM

    Amazon provides an additional $1.5 million to support National Capital Region food banks

    Amazon is dedicated to ensuring communities have reliable access to food year-round. As part of our overall commitment, we’ve donated an additional $1.5 million+ to help local food banks in the Northern Virginia and Washington D.C. metro area to meet increased demand. Throughout 2025, we’ve also used our transportation network to deliver more than 1.5 million meals to local organizations tackling food insecurity and have donated more than 2.9 million meals to help those experiencing hunger in the National Capitol Region.
    "With recent demand on local food banks reaching new levels, supporting reliable access to nutrition has never been more critical,” said Bettina Stix, Amazon’s global director of community impact. “By combining these donations with Amazon’s free home delivery and bulk delivery services offered to local food banks, we’re helping our partners meet immediate needs while also strengthening the region’s food system.”
    The contributions will support key regional organizations addressing food insecurity, including Capital Area Food Bank,Bread for the City, Arlington Food Assistance Center, DC Central Kitchen, Martha's Table, and So Others May Eat. These funds will help provide thousands of families with nutritious meals, holiday food assistance, and year-round hunger relief services.
    “Amazon’s contribution to our Holiday Helpings will provide over 18,500 local families with turkeys and other trimmings for the Thanksgiving holiday,” said Ashley Domm, Chief Development Officer, Bread for the City. “We originally planned to support 15,000 families this year, but because of generosity like Amazon's, we were able to go the extra mile. We are happy that Amazon was able to again provide this for our community this year.”
    Amazon helps food banks reach more people using our established last-mile infrastructure and logistics expertise. On top of financial donations, our delivery network—including Delivery Service Partners, Amazon Flex, and Amazon Shipping—has delivered more than 60 million meals directly to over 200,000 families experiencing food insecurity across the United States since 2020.
    We also provide bulk transportation services for free, moving tens of thousands of pounds of food weekly between suppliers, food banks, and community distribution points using our fleet of vehicles. In addition, our grocery stores provide excess food donations to food banks around the region. Together, these efforts help ensure food reaches families in need regardless of transportation costs, barriers, or mobility challenges.
    Learn more about Amazon’s commitment to food security.

    How Amazon is helping communities in Jamaica impacted by Hurricane Melissa

    Amazon is working closely with UN agencies and other first responders as Melissa threatens widespread devastation.

    October 31, 2025 8:30 AM

    Amazon welcomes DC Startup & Tech Week 2025 at HQ2

    Amazon proudly partnered with DC Startup & Tech Week (DCSTW) and Arlington Economic Development (AED) to celebrate the special tenth anniversary of this influential conference, hosting a full day of programming at HQ2. DCSTW has been a catalyst for regional innovation, creating vital connections between investors, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders across the DMV area and beyond.
    On Thursday, October 23, Amazon HQ2 hosted a full day of programming focused on innovation, entrepreneurship, and the future of work. As a leading sponsor alongside AED, Amazon facilitated sessions that strengthened ties with the region’s top innovators and local organizations.
    “We’re thrilled to partner with DC Startup & Tech Week,” said Patrick Phillippi, Head of Community Engagement for Greater Washington at Amazon. “Hosting the region’s leading founders and innovators at Amazon’s HQ2 reflects our deep commitment to empowering local entrepreneurship, fostering collaboration, and shaping the next chapter of innovation in the DMV.”
    HQ2 Event highlights included:
    • An exclusive networking session featuring 10 local startups pitching to investors
    • Founder workshops and investment discussions
    • Policy conversations led by Arlington Economic Development Director Ryan Touhill
    • Sessions exploring AI, automation, and emerging technologies
    • Innovation spotlights celebrating women leaders driving change in tech
    Throughout the day, discussions centered on artificial intelligence, automation, and the rapidly evolving technology landscape. Attendees engaged in dynamic fireside chats and policy conversations that highlighted the region’s growing startup ecosystem.
    This partnership exemplifies Amazon's commitment to investing in local communities—supporting founders, fostering innovation, and helping entrepreneurs transform ideas into impact.
    October 24, 2025 9:08 AM

    Celebrating Hispanic Heritage Month at HQ2: a season of music, flavor, and cultura

    At Amazon HQ2, Hispanic Heritage Month wasn’t just celebrated, it was lived. From lively evenings at Metropolitan Park to cultural showcases inside HQ2, each moment reflected the energy, connection, and creativity that define the Latino community across the DMV. Together with local partners, employees, and neighbors, Amazon spent the season amplifying voices, sharing stories, and honoring traditions that continue to shape our region.
    Every Thursday this fall, Metropolitan Park transformed into a vibrant gathering place for NaLa Noches, a Latin music series sponsored by Amazon and the National Landing Business Improvement District. Families, neighbors, and Amazonians came together as the park pulsed with rhythm and color, filled with boleros, salsa, and Venezuelan jazz from artists like Miramar, Alfredo Mojica, and Bio Ritmo. Guests enjoyed tacos and tres leches cake from local favorite Taqueria Xochi, along with Topo Chico Sabores, while the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington led hands-on art activities inspired by Día de los Muertos. The series captured what makes National Landing special—people from all walks of life gathering to celebrate culture and community.
    That same spirit carried inside HQ2, where the DMV Latinos employee-led group hosted Taste of Latin Cultura III, transforming the space into a celebration of music, flavor, and belonging. Amazonians heard from HQ2-based Latino leaders—including Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President of Public Policy, and Gonzalo de Dios, Head of Licensing & International Regulatory Affairs at Project Kuiper—alongside Michel Zajur, CEO of the Virginia Hispanic Chamber of Commerce, who spoke on representation, entrepreneurship, and culture’s role in innovation.
    The celebration continued through Expressions and Compass culinary workshops with Executive Chef George Gomez, where employees explored Latin flavors. The season may have ended, but the rhythm remains, in the community we support and the spaces it creates for cultures to shine.

    Amazon commits $1 billion to Career Choice employee education program as part of Future Ready 2030 fund

    The Future Ready 2030 fund supports students, employees, and those looking to expand their skills as the workforce adapts to a changing economy.

    October 20, 2025 10:08 AM

    AWS Skills Center Arlington celebrates 3 years of building a skilled HQ2 community

    Just steps away from Amazon’s HQ2 campus in National Landing, the AWS Skills Center Arlington is celebrating a major milestone: three years of empowering learners, growing the region’s tech workforce, and building community.
    Since opening in 2022, the AWS Skills Center has become a flagship investment in workforce development for HQ2, welcoming thousands of visitors and providing free cloud computing training to learners of all backgrounds—no prior experience required. Its mission aligns closely with Amazon’s broader commitment to making HQ2 not just a place to work, but a hub for economic opportunity and community impact across the DMV region.
    On October 23, 2025, the AWS Skills Center will host a full day of free, public events at 1550 – G Crystal Drive in Arlington, inviting the community to celebrate this milestone together.
    The day begins with “Give Back Together,” a community service project assembling hygiene kits for local families in need. Later in the afternoon, a “Vision & Voices” panel will feature distinguished organizations reflecting on the center’s journey and exploring future workforce development initiatives.
    The celebration continues later in the day with an inspiring learner panel, networking with industry professionals, delicious food, and exciting giveaways.
    As Amazon continues to grow its HQ2 presence, the AWS Skills Center plays a critical role in building a pipeline of local talent and expanding access to tech skills, ensuring that the opportunities created here benefit the entire community.
    The event is free and open to everyone. Join us as we celebrate three years of learning, impact, and community-building at HQ2’s doorstep.
    Register now on the site.
    October 13, 2025 1:17 PM

    Amazon HQ2 hosts Uniting Women in Cyber 2025

    Amazon HQ2 recently welcomed more than 250 leaders from government, industry, and the cybersecurity community to the WAS17 Event Center for Uniting Women in Cyber (UWIC) 2025. As a lead sponsor, Amazon was proud to provide a platform for experts to connect—through both relationships and technology, to strengthen global cyber preparedness and resilience.
    Throughout the day, participants engaged in deep technical discussions and thoughtful policy conversations on how to protect nations, organizations, and communities from evolving cyber threats. This year’s conference placed a spotlight on senior executives leading cybersecurity innovation across critical sectors.
    Amazon's Hart Rossman, vice president of Global Security Services at AWS, spoke on a panel that discussed cybersecurity collaboration in a globally interconnected economy.
    “AWS revolutionized computing with the invention of the world’s first public cloud nearly two decades ago. Today, millions of users build their businesses on our secure and compliant cloud and AI services, making us an integral part of the global supply chain. I was energized to connect with other leaders at this event and discuss how AWS’s Shared Responsibility Model and innovative cybersecurity strategies are transforming cloud security at global scale,” said Rossman.
    Amazon is deeply committed to empowering women in technology and advancing careers in cybersecurity. Through comprehensive internal programs, targeted mentorship opportunities, and initiatives led by our Women at Amazon employee-led group, we’re fostering an environment where women can thrive in technical roles and leadership positions.
    Located in the heart of the DMV, HQ2 continues to serve as a hub where public and private sector leaders come together to build partnerships that strengthen communities and drive progress. By partnering with UWIC, Amazon is helping to elevate diverse voices, spark meaningful collaboration, and advance cybersecurity innovation—reflecting our ongoing mission to be a good neighbor and employer in the region.

    Amazon employees can get health care for just $5 a week, with $5 doctor visits

    Amazon is investing over $1 billion to raise pay, lower health costs, and more for U.S. fulfillment and transportation employees.

    October 13, 2025 11:11 AM

    Amazon facility and employees near HQ2 recognized by Virginia Department for the Blind and Vision Impaired

    This National Disability Employment Awareness Month, an Amazon operations facility in Northern Virginia near the companies HQ2 offices was honored as Employer of the Year from the Virginia Department for the Blind and Vision Impaired (DBVI), highlighting Amazon’s ongoing efforts to be the earth’s most inclusive and accessible employer for people with disabilities.
    During a celebration event on Wednesday, October 8, the DBVI recognized Amazon’s RMN3 operations facility in Stafford, Virginia for the accommodations the facility’s team has made to empower blind and vision impaired employees. The recognition underscores Amazon’s commitment to building a diverse and inclusive workforce across the Northern Virginia region, where HQ2 is helping drive job creation and opportunity.
    At the same event, four of RMN3’s visually impaired employees, Mavis Williams, Alice Bickersteth, Antonette Johnson, Nacel Johnson, were also honored as the DBVI’s Employees of the Year. They discussed the positive impact Amazon’s efforts have on their livelihoods and how their jobs have afforded them to ability to lead independent lives.
    Amazon is proud to be named the DBVI’s Employer of the Year and is committed to investing in its employees in Northern Virginia and providing a safe and welcoming workplace.
    October 8, 2025 11:58 AM

    Amazon celebrates new STEM program and educational opportunities in southern Arlington

    Photo by Stephanie Lane
    Nearly three years into our partnership with Aspire, Amazon is proud to support the grand opening of Aspire's STEM Makerspace.
    On September 30, Amazon joined community leaders, educators, students, and families to celebrate the official opening of Aspire Afterschool Learning's Makerspace. The first no-cost community space for STEM programming in the area, the makerspace was created in response to the needs and input of the community.
    The new space expands STEM programming and hands-on learning opportunities, enables Aspire to support additional students in its programs, and provides a central meeting place for Aspire staff, students, and families.
    Amazon provided the initial funding for the project to enable Aspire to expand its facility and add the makerspace. With the new space, Aspire is incorporating science labs, coding classes, video and design projects, robotics demonstrations, structured play, and other interactive activities into its programming.
    Aspire served a record number of students during its 2024-2025 programs, with an average of 72% improving their math levels over the past two years. With community support, Aspire has sustained a 119% increase in enrollment over the last two years — in addition to expanding its fall STEM programming.
    "Amazon is honored to support Aspire's work to ensure that all children have the support they need from family, school, and the community," said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement at Amazon.
    With the opening of the Makerspace, Aspire is taking another step to bridge the "imagination gap" for historically underserved 3rd-8th-grade Arlington students through exposure to STEM fields.
    October 1, 2025 8:57 AM

    Amazon's Black Employee Network builds community at HQ2

    Evoto
    At Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington, the Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) is bringing our good neighbor mission to life—one event, one partnership, and one young leader at a time.
    This year, BEN-DMV partnered with Together at Amazon for their second annual Day of Service, joining forces with Everybody Wins DC (EWDC), The Distant Relatives Project, and Cardz for Kidz to promote youth literacy and support those experiencing homelessness. The event united more than 200 HQ2 volunteers for a day of service and impact.
    Volunteers assembled 1,000 hygiene kits, packed more than 500 books with personal notes to share with local schools, and created more than 600 cards for hospitalized children.
    "Representation through service means creating spaces where our communities can see, hear, and feel that they matter. Giving back locally is how we carry that forward, and the Tech Day of Service reflects the legacy we are building, one grounded in visibility, voice, and lasting impact," said Tyson J. Thompkins, director community engagement of BEN-DMV.
    BEN-DMV's impact goes far beyond one event. From cultural celebrations to neighborhood cleanups, the group builds programs that reflect HQ2's vision: a hub of innovation and inclusion that strengthens community.
    So far this year, they've hosted 59 events, partnered with more than 40 organizations and businesses, and contributed more than 3,600 volunteer hours—all while creating space for employees to lead with purpose and pride.
    "We're not just redefining what employee care looks like in the workplace—we're demonstrating how that same commitment can fuel meaningful change in the world around us," said Cash Ashley, president of BEN-DMV.
    As HQ2 grows, BEN-DMV is helping define what it means to show up for neighbors—with heart, culture, and intention.
    September 29, 2025 9:59 AM

    Community shines at Amazon HQ2's 2025 Community Day

    Met Park came alive over the weekend as Amazon's third annual HQ2 Community Day transformed our Arlington neighborhood into a vibrant festival of connection and creativity. The event wasn't just a celebration; it was our heartfelt thank-you note to the community for welcoming Amazon to the neighborhood.
    Imagine the scene: Children bouncing through a giant foam pit, their laughter mixing with the beats of a live DJ, while parents watched their little ones become temporary artists at the LEGO wall and face-painting stations. The air was sweet with cotton candy and filled with thousands of bubbles dancing in the autumn breeze.
    The Think Big Experience brought AWS technology to life, turning complex concepts into wonder. Young minds were captivated as they controlled robots, wrote their first lines of code, and marveled at The Science Guys of Baltimore's spectacular shows. It was science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) education disguised as entertainment!
    Food enthusiasts found their paradise at the Eat Loco Farmers Market and Met Park food venues, where local flavors took center stage. As families lounged on picnic blankets enjoying discounted treats, the festival atmosphere was perfect for an afternoon of community bonding.
    Amazon Alley showcased local entrepreneurship, featuring DMV-based businesses like Scarlettwares, Mocktail Club, and Bon AppeSweet. These innovative small business owners reminded us why our region's creative spirit is second to none.
    We are grateful to everyone who joined us and look forward to many more moments together.

    Amazon is investing over $1 billion to raise pay and lower health care costs for US fulfillment and transportation employees

    Average total compensation is now over $30 per hour when you include elected benefits.

    September 16, 2025 3:07 PM

    Arlington Community High School breaks ground at HQ2's Metropolitan Park

    On September 12, Amazon stood alongside Arlington Public Schools, county leaders, educators, and community partners to celebrate a milestone decade in the making—the unveiling of Arlington Community High School's (ACHS) future home at HQ2's Metropolitan Park. Starting in 2026, ACHS will finally have a home after years of operating without one.
    The celebration began with a warm reception catered by local restaurant Peruvian Brothers, setting the stage for an afternoon filled with community spirit. Leaders reflected on the significance of this moment—not only for ACHS students, but for Arlington as a whole.
    ACHS currently serves more than 300 students, many of whom balance work and family responsibilities while pursuing their high school diplomas. Their future home at HQ2 will offer more than classrooms.
    "This home is more than just a building," said Jeannette Allen, principal of Arlington Community High School. "It is a place where our students can feel stability, belonging and pride. It is a place where we can deepen our roots, strengthen our programs and build lasting partnerships with Amazon and this amazing and thriving business and education community."
    For Amazon, the ACHS partnership builds on a long-standing investment in the region. Since announcing Arlington as the site of HQ2, the company has contributed more than $239 million to local nonprofits, schools, and organizations.
    "When we chose Arlington as our home for our second headquarters, we committed to being more than just a neighbor—we wanted to be a true community partner," said Stephen Hartell, vp of public policy at Amazon. "We couldn't be more thrilled to welcome Arlington Community High School students into the heart of our HQ2 campus. This partnership represents exactly what we envisioned with HQ2—creating meaningful connections that strengthen our community."
    The unveiling of ACHS's new home is more than a building announcement—it's the next chapter in a story about resilience, opportunity, and the power of community. Together, Amazon and Arlington are proving that strong schools build strong communities.
    September 13, 2025 7:01 AM

    Amazon HQ2 serves up sweet summer fun with Banana Split Day

    On Monday, August 25, 2025, in Arlington, Amazon HQ2 brought neighbors together for one of the sweetest traditions of the summer—Banana Split Day at Metropolitan Park.
    The community gathered around Amazon's iconic banana stand to enjoy free banana splits, crafted with local favorite Toby's Homemade Ice Cream, complete with all the toppings to make each scoop a masterpiece. Live music from talented local performers filled the park, and the Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA) hosted creative, family-friendly activities that kept the afternoon lively and colorful.
    Banana Split Day is more than a summertime treat, it's part of Amazon HQ2's commitment to being a good neighbor, fostering community connection, and supporting local businesses and cultural organizations in National Landing. Partnerships with small businesses like Toby's and nonprofits like MoCA reflect our dedication to creating moments that bring people together while contributing to the local economy and arts scene.
    The event continued a tradition that began at Amazon's Seattle headquarters and has now become an HQ2 favorite. Judging by the smiles, the laughter, and the empty banana peels, it's safe to say this year's celebration was a hit. Toby's gave out 1,229 scoops to employees and community members, with over 300 served within the first hour!
    Thank you to our partners, neighbors, and everyone who joined us to make Banana Split Day 2025 a celebration to remember. We look forward to welcoming you back to Metropolitan Park for more events that connect, inspire, and celebrate our Arlington community.
    September 8, 2025 9:53 AM

    Amazon employees 'Stuff the Bus' with Salvation Army to support students

    Last month, Amazon HQ2 joined WJLA-TV and The Salvation Army for the annual Stuff the Bus back-to-school drive—united by one goal: helping local students start the school year ready to succeed.
    The event was a great success. More than 2,100 items were collected, with many community members also making online donations through 7News' Amazon Wish List. "We've been here three years now since we opened our headquarters. We have really made a commitment to be a good neighbor and a good partner to this region," said Patrick Phillippi, senior manager of community engagement.
    Amazon's momentum in Northern Virginia continues to grow. With more than 8,300 employees now based at HQ2, leaders underscored the company's dedication to investing in the community. "It's important for us to be involved here. We've got 8,300 employees at this beautiful facility, and we've been part of the community from day one," said Stephen Hartell, vice president of U.S. public policy.
    Partnering with The Salvation Army reflects Amazon's commitment to long-term impact. Stuff the Bus goes beyond backpacks and school supplies—it's an investment in children's education, their confidence, and the future of the region.
    We are grateful to everyone who donated, volunteered, and spread the word. Your generosity shows how Amazon HQ2's commitment to strengthening community comes alive through meaningful action. Together, we're ensuring that students and families start the school year with the tools they need to thrive.
    September 1, 2025 8:38 AM

    Amazon teams up with NFL, Washington Commanders, and Smithsonian for STEM education initiative

    The National Air and Space Museum set the stage for an evening where sports, science, and technology intersected to create a unique learning experience for local students. As part of Amazon's Thursday Night Football for Good initiative, the company joined forces with the Washington Commanders, NFL, and the Smithsonian to host an innovative STEM education event for Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington members.
    The event, part of the TNF for Good initiative, showed how sports can be a powerful catalyst for educational engagement. Local students from the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington received books and STEM kits to continue their educational journey beyond the museum walls. The event is part of a broader initiative to distribute 1 million books and educational resources to children across all 30 NFL markets this year.
    "Through Amazon's TNF for Good initiative, we're not just distributing books—we're distributing dreams, possibilities, to empower future leaders," said Brian Kenner, director of community engagement, Amazon.
    The joint effort represents a shared commitment to education and opportunity in the National Capital Region, where Amazon's HQ2 has become an active community partner. By bringing together the Smithsonian's educational resources, the NFL's Inspire Change program, and the Boys & Girls Clubs' community connections, we're creating opportunities for local youth to engage with STEM in new ways.
    This powerful partnership between cultural institutions, sports organizations, corporate entities, and community groups demonstrates what's possible when diverse organizations unite behind a common goal. The evening stood as testament to the impact that collaborative community engagement can have in inspiring the next generation of explorers, inventors, and innovators.
    August 25, 2025 3:38 PM

    Opening doors to the next generation of builders at HQ2

    In 2024 a small idea took root: How can Amazon open our doors to inspire the next generation of builders? That vision came to life when iD Tech, in partnership with ProServe, AWS Security and the Inclusive eXperiences and Technology team, launched the inaugural HQ2 Summer Camp, welcoming Amazon employees and local youth into a world of hands-on STEM learning.
    The two-week program offered a timely solution for Amazon builders balancing work and family schedules during the summer. HQ2 transformed into a buzzing hub of creativity where students aged 7–17 explored game design, artificial intelligence, Python programming, and robotics. Campers also toured our HQ2 offices, joined leadership for career chats, and expressed their creativity through Amazon’s “Expression Lab” projects. Mornings focused on coding, robotics development, and game design. Afternoons brought team-building games, social activities, and robotics competitions.
    What made the camp unique was the direct access to Amazon tech leaders who shared real-world insights, showing how harnessing curiosity can inspire innovation with world changing impact. By the end, campers had built functional games, AI models, and robotics prototypes, leaving with project files, learning transcripts, new skills, confidence, and an ignited passion for technology.
    For Amazon employees, the camp was a way to share their workplace with their families. “My child loved every minute, and I appreciated being able to share part of my day with them,” one parent said. A camper called HQ2 “the best office in the whole world.”

    Amazon has upskilled over 700,000 employees globally through prepaid education and training programs

    Amazon is enhancing its Career Choice education program globally and launching new robotics apprenticeships in the U.S.

    The program advances Amazon’s mission to foster innovation, strengthen local partnerships, and develop future talent. Through this initiative, Amazon continues its commitment to support employees' families, enrich our community, and inspire the next generation of innovators.
    August 4, 2025 11:58 AM

    Amazon's employee jazz ensemble hits all the right notes at HQ2

    Photo by Hiep Le
    At Amazon's HQ2, culture isn't just curated, it's created by our incredible employees who bring it to life every day. Recently, that spirit hit a high note as HQBlue, an eight-member jazz band made up entirely of Amazon HQ2 employees, delivered a soulful lunchtime performance at Met Park featuring classics like "Fly Me to the Moon" and "Autumn Leaves."
    What began as casual jam sessions among coworkers quickly grew into something more. The group, made up of engineers and legal professionals, rehearsed during lunch breaks and after hours, building harmony across teams and titles. Their debut drew a crowd of more than 100 Amazon employees, turning an ordinary afternoon into a celebration of creativity, connection, and soul.
    Moments like this showcase what makes HQ2 more than just an office. It's a community where people are encouraged to bring their full selves to work, including their hidden talents. Whether launching a product or playing jazz, employees are shaping a workplace where multidimensional talent is welcomed and celebrated.
    As Amazon deepens its roots in Arlington, Virginia, it remains committed to building a vibrant environment that fosters collaboration, joy, and spontaneous connection. HQBlue is just one example of how return-to-office at HQ2 isn't about going back, it's about moving forward together.
    At HQ2, employees' passions don't have to stay after hours. They can take the stage.
    June 27, 2025 10:27 AM

    Amazon’s Global Month of Volunteering at HQ2

    At Amazon, we believe being a good neighbor means showing up for the communities where we live and work. This commitment comes to life each year during our Global Month of Volunteering—a company-wide effort to support the people and places that surround us.
    This May, more than 2,200 employees in HQ2 joined forces to support more than 100 local nonprofits and community partners across Arlington, Northern Virginia, and Washington, D.C. From Met Park to National Landing, teams came together to plant gardens, pack essential supplies, clean local parks, and build meaningful connections with their neighbors.
    Whether it was new hires teaming up with long-time Amazon employees, or employees from different departments working side-by-side for the first time, one thing was clear: service creates community.
    These moments of impact, both big and small, left a lasting impression. Many of the organizations we worked with shared how much they appreciated the energy and commitment HQ2 volunteers brought to each project.
    While Global Month of Volunteering may be over, our commitment to the community continues year-round. HQ2 is home to people who care deeply about their colleagues, their neighbors, and the future of the region we all share.

    9 ways Amazon employees volunteered during our 2025 Global Month of Volunteering

    Here’s how Amazon employees support organizations and local communities around the world.

    Thank you to everyone who gave their time and energy this month. Your efforts remind us that volunteering isn’t just a one-time event, it’s a cornerstone of what it means to be part of HQ2.
    June 18, 2025 3:34 PM

    Warriors@Amazon: Service, community, and commitment at HQ2

    At Amazon's HQ2, being a good neighbor means showing up with humility, consistency, and purpose. One powerful example of this commitment is Warriors@Amazon, an employee-led group made up of veterans, military spouses, and allies who are using their shared dedication to service to strengthen ties with the local community.
    Whether through volunteerism, education, or support for those in need, Warriors@Amazon is helping to shape Amazon's presence in the National Capital Region in a way that honors the past while investing in the future.

    The National Memorial Day parade celebration

    This May, Warriors@Amazon proudly participated in the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C., as part of Amazon's Global Month of Volunteering. The event, one of the nation's most visible tributes to fallen service members, offered a meaningful opportunity for HQ2 employees to honor those who served and continue to serve.
    For many in the group, it was a deeply personal experience, one that reflected both Amazon's support of veterans and its role as a community partner in the region.

    Growing for good: Urban agriculture at Arlington House

    Just minutes from HQ2's offices, a quiet act of service is taking root. Each month from spring through fall, Warriors@Amazon volunteers and partners with the Capital Region Sustainability chapter to tend the kitchen garden at Arlington House, part of Arlington National Cemetery. In collaboration with the National Park Service and Plot Against Hunger, the produce harvested here is donated to local food banks, supporting neighbors across Northern Virginia.
    This project blends history, sustainability, and community service. Dozens of Amazon employees—many with military connections—volunteer their time to grow food that helps nourish families while preserving one of the area's most meaningful landmarks.

    Building community, together

    From participating in the Race Across America to supporting local veteran-focused initiatives, Warriors@Amazon's efforts reflect Amazon HQ2's broader vision: to be a responsible and engaged community partner.
    As HQ2 continues to grow, Amazon remains focused on investing in the region—not just through jobs and infrastructure, but through people, relationships, and long-term community impact.
    Warriors@Amazon embodies that mission. Their work is consistent, compassionate, and deeply connected to the communities Amazon serves. Through groups like Warriors@Amazon, HQ2 is building more than a physical presence, it's building trust, connection, and shared purpose. Together, we're growing stronger, one act of service at a time.

    Our Impact

    At Amazon, we're using our scale and abilities to make a positive impact in communities.

    April 25, 2025 9:47 AM

    Supporting Virginia Tech’s creativity and innovation at HQ2

    At Amazon, we’re proud to support Virginia Tech’s Institute for Creativity, Arts, and Technology (ICAT) as they bring innovative, creative experiences to life. This year, we launched a partnership with the institute to provide opportunities for collaboration, offering our Expressions Studio space at Met Park to host workshops for employees and community members alike—showcasing how creativity and technology can thrive together.
    Since August 2024, we’ve hosted workshops for more than 100 employees and community members. Participants engaged in hands-on sessions designed to spark creativity and encourage fresh approaches to problem-solving. These experiences allowed them to step away from their daily routines and explore new ways of thinking—transforming food waste into art and discovering other unique forms of creative expression.
    Everything was thoughtfully planned, and the welcoming atmosphere made it so easy to learn and engage. The opportunity to explore sustainable art techniques with Hiromi Okumura and visit Saya Behnam’s studio was truly inspiring. I appreciate all the effort you put into curating such a meaningful event, and I look forward to attending more workshops in the future.

    Sakina Akbar

    Participant
    Beyond the workshops, the program included a visit to the Torpedo Factory Art Center, a renowned local venue known for its emphasis on innovation and artistic collaboration. By facilitating this visit, we helped further connect employees with the local art scene—reinforcing the idea that creativity is a vital component of innovation.
    Amazon’s HQ2 thrives on our commitment to fostering a culture of creativity and community. While Virginia Tech leads the way, we’re proud to be a supportive partner in bringing these ideas to life—helping to build an environment where both technology and the arts can flourish.
    Through partnerships like this, we’re supporting initiatives that push boundaries, inspire new ideas, and enrich the broader community. It’s just one example of how Amazon HQ2 works behind the scenes to amplify creative efforts that shape a brighter, more innovative future.
    February 26, 2025 2:40 PM

    Leadership Spotlight: Amazonians driving innovation and growth at HQ2

    HQ2 is a true headquarters for Amazon and a wide array of business units and teams work out of Met Park. Our more than 8,000 employees span departments including Prime Video, Public Policy, Amazon Web Services (AWS), HR, and more. They each contribute unique perspectives that shape the company’s future.
    Here’s a look at a few leaders driving Amazon’s vision forward:

    Francessca Vasquez, Vice President, AWS Professional Services and Generative AI Innovation Center

    A technology leader with over 25 years of industry experience, Vasquez currently drives AWS's global consulting and product services across 48 countries, managing P&L and customer engagement for public sector, commercial, and partner operations. She excels in guiding organizations through generative AI transformations, leveraging AWS's AI/ML capabilities to design and deploy innovative solutions from startup to enterprise scale. Vasquez has been instrumental in shaping AWS's approach to delivering world-class solutions globally since joining in 2017.

    Kelly Day, Vice President, Prime Video International

    Kelly Day leads Prime Video’s international business, overseeing teams across 20+ countries who are focused on making Prime Video the first-stop entertainment destination for customers worldwide. Her role expanded in 2024 to include MGM+ and MGM Scripted Television teams. Day’s strategy integrates content, marketing, and partnerships to enhance the value Prime Video delivers to over 200 million Prime members globally.

    Ashleigh de la Torre, Vice President, Public Policy

    Ashleigh de la Torre leads a Public Policy team supporting Amazon’s U.S. operations, logistics, and delivery businesses, and Amazon’s sustainability efforts. She and her team engage lawmakers and regulators at the local, state and federal levels on behalf of the company and our customers. Her work includes policymaker advocacy to improve transportation safety, support continued investment in transportation infrastructure, and encourage development of innovative and sustainable new technologies and delivery solutions.

    Rick Freeman, Vice President, Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS)

    With over 20 years of experience in defense and space industries, Rick oversees Kuiper Government Solutions (KGS), which will provide secure broadband services and space network solutions for government customers worldwide. KGS is the government-focused division of Project Kuiper, an Amazon initiative to deliver fast, reliable, and secure connectivity through a constellation of satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO). As a U.S. Marine Corps veteran, he is passionate about providing reliable satellite communications for mission-critical applications.

    Eric Orme, Vice President, Live Sports Product and Engineering, Prime Video

    Eric Orme has played a pivotal role in developing Prime Video’s live sports and entertainment strategy. His team delivers high-quality live experiences for global audiences, including events like the English Premier League and Thursday Night Football. Orme focuses on personalizing the sports viewing experience while ensuring the successful operation of every live event.

    Christine Enemark, VP & AGC, Prime Video

    Over 13 years ago, when Christine joined Amazon, there were 3 lawyers supporting a new video streaming business operating in the United States. Today, she leads a global legal team of over 85 professionals across 14 countries, supporting Prime Video’s expansion into movies, TV shows, Amazon Originals, unscripted content, and live sports. She encourages her team to advise with the end customer in mind, shaping a legal strategy that aligns with Amazon’s customer-first approach. This perspective has strengthened both the team and the business.

    Jay Shankar, Vice President, Global Talent Acquisition

    Jay Shankar combines her engineering background with a customer-centric approach to recruiting. Since joining Amazon, she has led talent acquisition for key businesses like AWS, Advertising, and Prime Video. Shankar’s focus is on leveraging technology and data analytics to streamline recruiting processes and build a robust global talent pipeline. She also prioritizes inclusivity and career development, ensuring Amazon remains a leader in attracting top talent.
    February 4, 2025 12:02 PM

    Love is in the air: Celebrate Valentine’s Day with Amazon HQ2 at Met Park!

    This Valentine’s Day, Amazon HQ2 is teaming up with local favorites like Calluna Flower Truck, The Museum of Contemporary Art Arlington (MoCA), Brightly Ever After, and a variety of Met Park restaurants for a festive celebration of love and community in Arlington, Virginia. As a proud neighbor, we're excited to bring everyone together with family-friendly events that celebrate the spirit of the season.
    Free roses and cardmaking at MoCA: MoCA is spreading the love with special giveaways and activities! From 11 a.m. ET to 2 p.m. ET, Calluna Flower Truck will hand out 500 free roses outside MoCA to both Amazonians and community members. If you're looking for the perfect gift, beautiful bouquets will also be available for purchase. After picking up your rose, head inside MoCA for a fun Cardmaking Workshop. Their team will help you craft heartfelt notes for your loved ones.
    Food pop-ups at Met Park: No celebration is complete without great food! On February 14, we’ll have a variety of delicious restaurant pop-ups at Met Park. Whether you're out with a friend or a special someone, come sample tasty treats and snacks to add flavor to your day.
    Valentine’s Day decorations at Met Park: To set the mood, we’re giving Met Park a romantic makeover with Valentine’s-themed sidewalk details and stunning floral decorations. Thanks to Brightly Ever After, the Pergolas near the dog park will be beautifully adorned, making it the perfect spot for a stroll or a photo op. Keep an eye out for vibrant decals leading you to the “I Heart Met Park” campaign page!
    Spread the love! Amazon HQ2 is committed to fostering creativity, connection, and community in Arlington. By partnering with local businesses like Calluna Flower Truck and Brightly Ever After, we’re helping make Met Park a place where everyone can come together and celebrate. We can't wait to celebrate Valentine’s Day with you—see you at Met Park!

    Amazon Prime members enjoyed fastest-ever delivery speeds in 2024, and US members saved on average over $500 on delivery fees

    Celebree School of National Landing, Good Company Doughnuts &amp; Cafe, Glo30, and Peruvian Brothers are the latest businesses signed on to open in Amazon’s second headquarters in Arlington, Virginia.

    January 29, 2025 11:55 AM

    Celebrating minority entrepreneurs at Amazon’s HQ2

    January 29th marks Minority Entrepreneur Day, a time to honor the contributions of minority-owned businesses. HQ2’s retailers are transforming the culinary landscape with unique flavors and cultural pride.

    Amazon is increasing the average total compensation package for fulfillment and transportation employees in the US to over $29 per hour

    Our biggest ever investment in pay and benefits—a total of more than $2.2 billion across our network.

    Peruvian Brothers: Founded by brothers Giuseppe and Mario Lanzone, Peruvian Brothers serves bold, authentic Peruvian cuisine. From their food truck beginnings to a popular HQ2 restaurant, their success is built on fresh ingredients, tradition, and community connection.
    “From Day 1 twelve years ago, our motto has been, “we’re more than just a food truck; it’s about creating a community where our staff knows people by name, remembers their favorite orders, and shares the best parts of our Peruvian cuisine and culture through experiences like Alpaca Happy Hours.” — Giuseppe Lanzone, Co-Founder & Chief Operating Officer
    Toby’s is known for ice cream with a modern twist, blending international flavors into a locally-sourced menu. Their commitment to quality and community has made it a local favorite and a symbol of perseverance.
    “As a minority business owner, sometimes I feel we are overlooked by the community. Amazon has been very supportive every step of the way, and takes every opportunity to include us in their events. We are looking forward to a long, fruitful partnership and prosperous 2025.” Toby Bantug, Owner
    Taqueria Xochi specializes in authentic Mexican street food, from tacos to tamales. Xochi’s passion for tradition and innovation makes this spot a hub for Mexican culture and hospitality.
    “We’re thrilled to bring the vibrant, authentic flavors of Mexican cuisine to Amazon HQ2. Beyond serving great food and drinks, our goal is to provide a welcoming space where employees and the local community can gather, connect, and feel truly at home.” — Geraldine Mendoza, Director of Operations & Partner
    Makers Union offers a fun menu perfect for any occasion, including pet-friendly patio lunches. As part of one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food companies, it thrives in the HQ2 community.
    Photo by @FOODSINMYHEART
    “Our fun and approachable menu is perfect for your next happy hour, business dinner, or lunch with your pup on the pet-friendly patio. Makers Union is proudly owned and operated by Thompson Restaurants, one of the nation’s largest minority-owned food and facilities management companies.” — Warren Thompson, President and Chairman

    Amazon named among the world’s most admired companies by ‘Fortune’ magazine for the ninth consecutive year

    The comprehensive study recognizes companies for the innovation and value they bring to their customers, employees, and communities.

    January 13, 2025 8:04 AM

    A year in review at HQ2: Continuing to deliver for our communities

    2024 was an exciting year for us and our partners in the National Capital Region. As we celebrate the new year, we want to take a moment to reflect on our collective impact, express gratitude to our neighbors and community partners who continue to welcome us with open arms, and acknowledge the work of so many Amazon employees who are working hard to serve our customers and communities.
    In 2024, we welcomed three new small businesses, and we now have 14 retailers open at Met Park—most of which are women and minority-owned. Local businesses are already experiencing a 13% increase in foot traffic and 1 million additional visits to the region since HQ2 opened its doors, and we’re excited for this growth to continue as Amazon employees work from the office more frequently in 2025.
    We remain deeply committed to the National Capital Region, donating more than $78 million to community groups and nonprofits in 2024 alone. As a result, we were honored to be named the top corporate philanthropist for the second year in a row by the Washington Business Journal.
    We were also proud to receive LEED Platinum v4 certification from the U.S. Green Building Council, making Amazon HQ2 the largest newly constructed building in the world to achieve this distinction. This milestone reinforces our commitment to sustainability, while representing a meaningful step towards decarbonizing Amazon’s real estate portfolio in order to meet our Climate Pledge commitment.
    As we enter the new year, we’re inspired by the innovation, collaboration, and connection that we’ve already seen with our teams working in person together. With most employees working from the office each day, we look forward to continuing to support local small businesses while contributing to economic growth and the overall vibrancy of National Landing.
    We are incredibly proud of everything our teammates are accomplishing, as well as our ongoing commitment to our customers, employees, and the communities we call home. Here's to an exciting 2025 and beyond—Amazon will continue to strive to be a good neighbor and trusted business partner in the region.

    How Amazon is helping communities impacted by the California wildfires

    Amazon is committing $10 million to relief efforts in addition to continuing to provide essential items through its Disaster Relief hubs.

    December 13, 2024 5:29 AM

    BEN-DMV hosts third annual 'Great Big Kid' event, celebrating largest Amazon-Boys & Girls Club partnership

    Amazon's Black Employee Network (BEN-DMV) recently celebrated a monumental milestone in its ongoing partnership with the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington, hosting the third annual "Great Big Kid" event at Amazon's HQ2. This year’s celebration was the largest and most impactful to date, highlighting the continued growth and success of this powerful initiative.
    The “Great Big Kid” program, a six-week mentorship initiative, pairs Amazon volunteers with local youth, providing them with invaluable opportunities for personal and professional growth.
